text:
Three hundred years. Seventeen crises. Each one came with warnings nobody wanted to hear.

This is Economic Crises Explained.

We begin in 1720 with the South Sea Bubble — the first great financial mania of the modern era — and follow the wreckage forward: through hyperinflation and depression, through lost decades and debt crises, all the way to the crash of 2008 and the inflation shock of 2021.

For every crisis, the same four questions: what lit the fuse, why no one stopped it, how governments responded, and what it ultimately cost. The answers are almost never comfortable.

Seventeen episodes. Three hundred years. One argument that history has been making the whole time.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode five — The Great Depression.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode six — The 1970s Stagflation.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode ten — The Asian Financial Crisis.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode fourteen — The Global Financial Crisis.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode sixteen — The COVID-19 Economic Shock.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode seventeen — The Great Inflation.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode four — The Weimar Hyperinflation.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode nine — The Japanese Lost Decade.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode eleven — The LTCM Collapse.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode fifteen — The Eurozone Debt Crisis.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode three — The Panic of 1907.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode seven — The Latin American Debt Crisis.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode eight — Black Monday.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode twelve — The Dot-com Crash.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode thirteen — The Argentine Crisis.
Welcome back to Economic Crises Explained. Episode two — The Long Depression.
This is Economic Crises Explained. Episode one — The South Sea Bubble.

The patterns repeat. The faces change. The cost is always borne by those who had no say.

Economic Crises Explained. 2026.
